index.name: Nifty50 Value 20 Index
index.description: Nifty50 Value 20 is a smart-beta 'factor index' that selects the 20 stocks from Nifty 50 with the highest composite 'value score' — a proprietary NSE formula weighting Return on Capital Employed (ROCE), Price-to-Earnings (PE), Price-to-Book (PB), and Dividend Yield. The underlying philosophy is Benjamin Graham's Value Investing: buy fundamentally strong companies at cheap prices and let mean reversion deliver alpha. In India's growth-focused market, value investing has historically struggled to consistently outperform quality/growth strategies because Indian markets reward high-growth companies with persistent premium multiples. However, value ETFs like NV20BEES shine during market corrections and rate-rising cycles — when expensive growth stocks get de-rated but cheap industrials, PSU banks and commodity stocks hold or outperform. The 20-stock construct means very high concentration risk — a single stock can be 8-12% of the index, amplifying sector-specific or stock-specific impact. NV20BEES typically holds PSU banks, ITC, coal/oil PSUs, and value-screened Nifty 50 cyclicals that trade below historical P/B or P/E. It's a tactical/satellite allocation tool rather than a core portfolio holding — investors use it as a 10-15% value tilt overlay on a main Nifty 50 or multi-cap holding. Nippon India AMC is one of India's oldest mutual fund houses with strong ETF product management capabilities.
